tools.name: search
tools.description: Full-text search across the Estonian court-decision corpus (civil, administrative and criminal rulings from Riigi Teataja, indexed by lahend.ee). Inflection-tolerant; wrap a phrase in "quotes" for an exact match. Returns matches each with a stable `id` (pass to `fetch` or `get_ruling`) and a canonic

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Retrieve the full text + metadata of a ruling by the `id` returned from `search` (e.g. 'ruling:1279503'). Returns the decision as Markdown with its linked companies and canonical lahend.ee URL.

tools.name: search_rulings
tools.description: Full-text ruling search with optional filters: court name, case type (civil/administrative/criminal), area of law (asja_kategooria) and a decision-date range. Returns a page of rulings with a total count. Use `list_courts` / `list_categories` to discover valid filter values.

tools.name: rulings_by_company
tools.description: Given an 8-digit Estonian registry code (registrikood), return every court decision that names that company, with its role (claimant/defendant/etc.) and the other parties. This company↔case-law join is unique to lahend.ee. Find a registry code via nimistu.ee or the nimistu MCP.

tools.name: list_courts
tools.description: All Estonian courts that have published decisions, each with its ruling count. Use a court name as the `court` filter in `search_rulings`.

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description: All areas of law (asja_kategooria) present in the corpus, each with its ruling count. Use a category as the `category` filter in `search_rulings`.

tools.name: recent_rulings
tools.description: The newest published rulings (by decision date), across all courts and areas of law.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.
